query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.218 (commit: b0d37589).

## Issue to Resolve

User report: Can't see or open CSVs
### Checked for duplicates?

- [x] This issue is not a duplicate

### Does it also happen in the desktop version?

- [x] This bug does not occur in the latest version of Anki Desktop

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

Exact steps unknown

https://www.reddit.com/r/Anki/comments/1i8b5p3/best_way_to_easily_create_flashcards_on_ankidroid/

User is on Discord and responsive: https://discord.com/channels/368267295601983490/701939078786187315/1332453507789357116

Requested a screenshot of the file in the location, and a screenshot of AnkiDroid not picking it up


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/429cc47f-d727-4121-b7d9-e11de1788a81)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9bd58b28-7363-466c-880a-6145a9a5b508)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e7be5d0c-2a20-4369-8e56-d63d07fbaa76)



### Expected behaviour

The CSV file is visible

### Debug info

```text
AnkiDroid Version = 2.20.1 (e32a82c33646807ea19623c9c88be38965bc07e6)
Backend Version = 0.1.48-anki24.11 (24.11 c47638ca36f99dd4f3b81ae82d964aec66e392e0)
Android Version = 13 (SDK 33)
ProductFlavor = play
Manufacturer = Google
Model = zork
Hardware = bertha
Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; zork Build/R133-16151.13.0; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/131.0.6778.260 Safari/537.36
ACRA UUID = 2869b58f-542e-4631-b1a7-ec59807d7558
FSRS = 1.4.3 (Enabled: false)
Crash Reports Enabled = true
```

### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

While testing, I was having huge problems with 'saving' CSVs in Google Drive, but they didn't appear in the folder I saved them in.

I don't trust files on Android at all

### Research

- [x] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [x] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 21→<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
 22→    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"
 23→    android:installLocation="auto">
 24→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.camera" android:required="false" />
 25→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.camera.any" android:required="false" />
 26→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.audio.output" android:required="false" />
 27→    <uses-feature android:name="android.software.app_widgets" android:required="false" />
 28→    <uses-feature android:name="android.hardware.microphone" android:required="false" />
 29→    <!-- `ScopedStorage` lint error is ignored:
 30→        the recommended change is setting maxSDkVersion
 31→         but this means preserveLegacyExternalStorage is inactive, and users do not have access to
 32→         the ~/AnkiDroid public directory
 33→    -->
 34→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:ignore="ScopedStorage"/>
 35→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.VIBRATE" />
 36→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
 37→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE" />
 38→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO" />
 39→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WAKE_LOCK"/>
 40→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.GET_PACKAGE_SIZE" android:maxSdkVersion="25" />
 41→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED" />
 42→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE" />
 43→    <!-- Disabled on Play Store -->
 44→    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:ignore="ScopedStorage" />
 45→    <permission android:name="${applicationId}.permission.READ_WRITE_DATABASE"
 46→                android:label="@string/read_write_permission_label"
 47→                android:description="@string/read_write_permission_description"
 48→                android:protectionLevel="dangerous"
 49→                />
 50→
 51→    <supports-screens
 52→        android:anyDensity="true"
 53→        android:largeScreens="true"
 54→        android:normalScreens="true"
 55→        android:resizeable="true"
 56→        android:smallScreens="true"
 57→        android:xlargeScreens="true"
 58→        />
 59→
 60→    <queries>
 61→        <intent>
 62→            <action android:name="android.intent.action.TTS_SERVICE" />
 63→        </intent>
 64→        <intent>
 65→            <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
 66→            <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
 67→            <data android:scheme="https" />
 68→        </intent>
 69→        <intent>
 70→            <action android:name="android.intent.action.PROCESS_TEXT" />
 71→            <data android:mimeType="text/plain" />
 72→        </intent>
 73→        <intent>
 74→            <action android:name="android.speech.RecognitionService" />
 75→        </intent>
 76→    </queries>
 77→
 78→    <!--
 79→       Attribute usesCleartextTraffic, added in API 23 and false by default if targeting API >= 28,
 80→       is ignored on Android 7 (API 24) and above if networkSecurityConfig is present.
 81→       https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/manifest/application-element#usesCleartextTraffic
 82→    -->
 83→    <application
 84→        android:name="com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp"
 85→        android:label="@string/app_name"
 86→        android:allowBackup="true"
 87→        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
 88→        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
 89→        android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"
 90→        android:usesCleartextTraffic="true"
 91→        android:largeHeap="true"
 92→        android:hasFragileUserData="true"
 93→        android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/network_security_config"
 94→        android:requestLegacyExternalStorage="true"
 95→        android:preserveLegacyExternalStorage="true"
 96→        android:resizeableActivity="true"
 97→        android:supportsRtl="true"
 98→        android:theme="@style/Theme_Light"
 99→        android:manageSpaceActivity="com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.managespace.ManageSpaceActivity"
100→        >
101→        <!-- Probably because of a bug on the framework,
102→        the full package path is needed for manageSpaceActivity -->
103→        <activity
104→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.managespace.ManageSpaceActivity"
105→            android:exported="true"/>
106→        <activity android:name=".DrawingActivity"
107→            android:label="@string/drawing"
108→            android:exported="false"
109→            android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize"
110→            />
111→        <activity
112→            android:name="com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler"
113→            android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize"
114→            android:theme="@android:style/Theme.Translucent.NoTitleBar"
115→            android:exported="true"
116→            >
117→            <intent-filter>
118→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
119→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
120→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.MULTIWINDOW_LAUNCHER" />
121→            </intent-filter>
122→            <!-- *.apkg matcher. NOTE: when pathPattern is used, host and scheme must also be specified -->
123→            <intent-filter>
124→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
125→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
126→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
127→                <!-- .apkg files -->
128→                <data
129→                    android:host="*"
130→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
131→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
132→                    android:scheme="http"
133→                    />
134→                <data
135→                    android:host="*"
136→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
137→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
138→                    android:scheme="https"
139→                    />
140→                <data
141→                    android:host="*"
142→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
143→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
144→                    android:scheme="content"
145→                    />
146→                <data
147→                    android:host="*"
148→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
149→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.apkg"
150→                    android:scheme="file"
151→                    />
152→                <!-- .colpkg files -->
153→                <data
154→                    android:host="*"
155→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
156→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
157→                    android:scheme="http"
158→                    />
159→                <data
160→                    android:host="*"
161→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
162→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
163→                    android:scheme="https"
164→                    />
165→                <data

167→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
168→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
169→                    android:scheme="content"
170→                    />
171→                <data
172→                    android:host="*"
173→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
174→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.colpkg"
175→                    android:scheme="file"
176→                    />
177→                <!-- tsv files -->
178→                <data
179→                    android:host="*"
180→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
181→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"
182→                    android:scheme="http"
183→                    />
184→                <data
185→                    android:host="*"
186→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
187→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"
188→                    android:scheme="https"
189→                    />

193→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"

199→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.tsv"
200→                    android:scheme="file"
201→                    />
202→                <!-- csv files -->
203→                <data
204→                    android:host="*"
205→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
206→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
207→                    android:scheme="http"
208→                    />
209→                <data
210→                    android:host="*"
211→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
212→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
213→                    android:scheme="https"
214→                    />
215→                <data

218→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
219→                    android:scheme="content"
220→                    />
221→                <data
222→                    android:host="*"
223→                    android:mimeType="*/*"
224→                    android:pathPattern=".*\\.csv"
225→                    android:scheme="file"
226→                    />
227→            </intent-filter>
228→            <!-- MIME type matcher for .apkg files coming from providers like gmail which hide the file extension -->
229→            <intent-filter>
230→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
231→                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
232→                <data android:mimeType="application/apkg"/>
233→                <data android:mimeType="application/colpkg"/>
234→                <data android:mimeType="application/vnd.anki"/>
235→                <data android:mimeType="application/x-apkg"/>
236→                <data android:mimeType="application/x-colpkg"/>
237→                <data android:mimeType="application/octet-stream"/>
238→                <data android:mimeType="application/zip"/>
239→                <data android:mimeType="text/tab-separated-values"/>
240→                <data android:mimeType="text/comma-separated-values"/>
241→            </intent-filter>
242→
243→            <!-- Keep it separate as when sharing image AnkiDroid would be shown with Image Occlusion label -->
244→            <intent-filter android:label="@string/image_occlusion">
245→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
246→                <action android:name="android.intent.action.SEND" />

 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.dialogs
 18→
 19→import android.app.Dialog
 20→import android.content.ActivityNotFoundException
 21→import android.content.Intent
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Parcelable
 24→import androidx.activity.result.ActivityResultLauncher
 25→import androidx.annotation.StringRes
 26→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 27→import androidx.core.os.BundleCompat
 28→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 29→import androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 34→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.TEXT_PLAIN
 36→import com.ichi2.utils.title
 37→import kotlinx.parcelize.Parcelize
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@NeedsTest("Selecting APKG does not allow multiple files")
 41→@NeedsTest("Selecting COLPKG does not allow multiple files")
 42→@NeedsTest("Restore backup dialog does not allow multiple files")
 43→class ImportFileSelectionFragment : DialogFragment() {
 44→    override fun onCreateDialog(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): Dialog {
 45→        val entries = buildImportEntries()
 46→        return AlertDialog
 47→            .Builder(requireActivity())
 48→            .title(R.string.menu_import)
 49→            .setItems(
 50→                entries.map { requireActivity().getString(it.titleRes) }.toTypedArray(),
 51→            ) { _, position ->
 52→                val entry = entries[position]
 53→                UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 54→                    UsageAnalytics.Category.LINK_CLICKED,
 55→                    entry.analyticsId,
 56→                )
 57→                openImportFilePicker(
 58→                    activity = requireActivity() as AnkiActivity,
 59→                    fileType = entry.type,
 60→                    multiple = entry.multiple,
 61→                    mimeType = entry.mimeType,
 62→                    extraMimes = entry.extraMimes,
 63→                )
 64→            }.create()
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    private fun buildImportEntries(): List<ImportEntry> {
 68→        return arguments?.let { args ->
 69→            args.classLoader = this@ImportFileSelectionFragment::class.java.classLoader
 70→            val options =
 71→                BundleCompat.getParcelable(args, ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS, ImportOptions::class.java)
 72→                    ?: return emptyList()
 73→            mutableListOf<ImportEntry>().apply {
 74→                if (options.importApkg) {
 75→                    add(
 76→                        ImportEntry(
 77→                            R.string.import_deck_package,
 78→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_APKG_FILE,
 79→                            ImportFileType.APKG,
 80→                        ),
 81→                    )
 82→                }
 83→                if (options.importColpkg) {
 84→                    add(
 85→                        ImportEntry(
 86→                            R.string.import_collection_package,
 87→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_COLPKG_FILE,
 88→                            ImportFileType.COLPKG,
 89→                        ),
 90→                    )
 91→                }
 92→                if (options.importTextFile) {
 93→                    add(
 94→                        ImportEntry(
 95→                            R.string.import_csv,
 96→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_CSV_FILE,
 97→                            ImportFileType.CSV,
 98→                            multiple = false,
 99→                            mimeType = "*/*",
100→                            extraMimes =
101→                                arrayOf(
102→                                    TEXT_PLAIN,
103→                                    "text/comma-separated-values",
104→                                    "text/csv",
105→                                    "text/tab-separated-values",
106→                                ),
107→                        ),
108→                    )
109→                }
110→            }
111→        } ?: emptyList()
112→    }
113→
114→    // safe as this data class is used as a container and it's not involved in any comparing
115→    @Suppress("ArrayInDataClass")
116→    private data class ImportEntry(
117→        @StringRes val titleRes: Int,
118→        val analyticsId: String,
119→        val type: ImportFileType,
120→        var multiple: Boolean = false,
121→        val mimeType: String = "*/*",
122→        val extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
123→    )
124→
125→    @Parcelize
126→    data class ImportOptions(
127→        val importColpkg: Boolean,
128→        val importApkg: Boolean,
129→        val importTextFile: Boolean,
130→    ) : Parcelable
131→
132→    enum class ImportFileType {
133→        APKG,
134→        COLPKG,
135→        CSV,
136→    }
137→
138→    interface ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider {
139→        fun getApkgFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
140→    }
141→
142→    interface CsvImportResultLauncherProvider {
143→        fun getCsvFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
144→    }
145→
146→    companion object {
147→        private const val ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS = "arg_import_options"
148→
149→        fun newInstance(options: ImportOptions) =
150→            ImportFileSelectionFragment().apply {
151→                arguments = bundleOf(ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS to options)
152→            }
153→
154→        /**
155→         * Calls through the system with an [Intent] to pick a file to be imported.
156→         */
157→        fun openImportFilePicker(
158→            activity: AnkiActivity,
159→            fileType: ImportFileType,
160→            multiple: Boolean = false,
161→            mimeType: String = "*/*",
162→            extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
163→        ) {
164→            Timber.d("openImportFilePicker() delegating to file picker intent")
165→            val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_OPEN_DOCUMENT)
166→            intent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_OPENABLE)
167→            intent.type = mimeType
168→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_ADVANCED", true)
169→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.FANCY", true)
170→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_FILESIZE", true)
171→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_ALLOW_MULTIPLE, multiple)
172→            extraMimes?.let { intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_MIME_TYPES, it) }
173→
174→            try {
175→                if (
176→                    (fileType == ImportFileType.APKG || fileType == ImportFileType.COLPKG) &&
177→                    activity is ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider
178→                ) {
179→                    activity.getApkgFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
180→                } else if (fileType == ImportFileType.CSV && activity is CsvImportResultLauncherProvider) {
181→                    activity.getCsvFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
182→                } else {
183→                    Timber.w("Activity($activity) can't handle requested import: $fileType")
184→                }
185→            } catch (ex: ActivityNotFoundException) {
186→                Timber.w("No activity to handle openImportFilePicker request")
187→                activity.showSnackbar(R.string.activity_start_failed)
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→}
192→

34→ *     at commit 1931e06ac0424ff5c7bbc2df34c658b9e95e11f6
35→ */
36→package com.ichi2.utils
37→
38→import android.net.Uri
39→import android.webkit.MimeTypeMap
40→import java.io.File
41→import java.util.Locale
42→
43→/** Clone of RestrictedApi functionality  */
44→object AssetHelper {
45→    /**
46→     Returns the extension of [path].
47→     It uses [MimeTypeMap.getFileExtensionFromUrl], with the path transformed into a Uri.
48→     */
49→    fun getFileExtensionFromFilePath(path: String): String =
50→        MimeTypeMap.getFileExtensionFromUrl(
51→            Uri.fromFile(File(path)).toString().lowercase(
52→                Locale.ROOT,
53→            ),
54→        )
55→
56→    /**
57→     * Use [MimeTypeMap.getMimeTypeFromExtension] to guess MIME type or return the
58→     * "text/plain" if it can't guess.
59→     *
60→     * Copy of [androidx.webkit.internal.AssetHelper.guessMimeType]
61→     *
62→     * @param path path of the file to guess its MIME type.
63→     * @return MIME type guessed from file extension or "text/plain".
64→     */
65→    fun guessMimeType(path: String): String =
66→        when (val extension = getFileExtensionFromFilePath(path)) {
67→            "js" -> "text/javascript"
68→            "mjs" -> "text/javascript"
69→            "json" -> "application/json"
70→            else -> MimeTypeMap.getSingleton().getMimeTypeFromExtension(extension) ?: TEXT_PLAIN
71→        }
72→
73→    /** Used for mime type or Intent type when sharing text via other applications **/
74→    const val TEXT_PLAIN = "text/plain"
75→}
76→

 53→/**
 54→ * Class which handles how the application responds to different intents, forcing it to always be single task,
 55→ * but allowing custom behavior depending on the intent
 56→ * It inherits from [AbstractIntentHandler]
 57→ *
 58→ * @author Tim
 59→ */
 60→class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
 61→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 62→        // Note: This is our entry point from the launcher with intent: android.intent.action.MAIN
 63→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 64→        val intent = intent
 65→        Timber.v(intent.toString())
 66→        val reloadIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
 67→        reloadIntent.setDataAndType(getIntent().data, getIntent().type)
 68→        val action = intent.action
 69→        // #6157 - We want to block actions that need permissions we don't have, but not the default case
 70→        // as this requires nothing
 71→        val runIfStoragePermissions = { runnable: () -> Unit -> performActionIfStorageAccessible(reloadIntent, action) { runnable() } }
 72→        val launchType = getLaunchType(intent)
 73→        // TODO block the UI with some kind of ProgressDialog instead of cancelling the sync work
 74→        if (requiresCollectionAccess(launchType)) {
 75→            SyncWorker.cancel(this)
 76→        }
 77→        when (launchType) {
 78→            LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT ->
 79→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 80→                    handleFileImport(fileIntent, reloadIntent, action)
 81→                    finish()
 82→                }
 83→            LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT ->
 84→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 85→                    onSelectedCsvForImport(fileIntent)
 86→                    finish()
 87→                }
 88→            LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT ->
 89→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 90→                    handleImageImport(intent)
 91→                    finish()
 92→                }
 93→            LaunchType.SYNC -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleSyncIntent(reloadIntent, action) }
 94→            LaunchType.REVIEW -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleReviewIntent(reloadIntent, intent) }
 95→            LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW -> {
 96→                Timber.d("onCreate() performing default action")
 97→                launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
 98→            }
 99→            LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> {
100→                copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent)
101→                finish()
102→            }
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent: Intent) {
107→        Timber.i("Copying debug info to clipboard")
108→        // null string is handled by copyToClipboard in try-catch
109→        this.copyToClipboard(
110→            text = (intent.getStringExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA)!!),
111→            failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info,
112→        )
113→    }
114→
115→    private val fileIntent: Intent
116→        get() {
117→            return if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
118→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Intent::class.java) ?: intent
119→            } else {
120→                intent
121→            }
122→        }
123→
124→    /**
125→     * Execute the runnable if one of the two following conditions are satisfied:
126→     *
127→     *  * AnkiDroid is using an app-private directory to store user data
128→     *  * AnkiDroid is using a legacy directory to store user data but has access to it since storage permission
129→     * has been granted (as long as AnkiDroid targeted API < 30, requested legacy storage, and has not been uninstalled since)
130→     *
131→     */
132→    @NeedsTest("clicking a file in 'Files' to import")
133→    private fun performActionIfStorageAccessible(
134→        reloadIntent: Intent,
135→        action: String?,
136→        block: () -> Unit,
137→    ) {
138→        if (grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
139→            Timber.i("User has storage permissions. Running intent: %s", action)
140→            block()
141→        } else {
142→            Timber.i("No Storage Permission, cancelling intent '%s'", action)
143→            launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun handleReviewIntent(
148→        reloadIntent: Intent,
149→        reviewerIntent: Intent,
150→    ) {
151→        val deckId = intent.getLongExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, 0)
152→        Timber.i("Handling intent to review deck '%d'", deckId)
153→
154→        val reviewIntent =
155→            if (sharedPrefs().getBoolean("newReviewer", false)) {
156→                ReviewerFragment.getIntent(this)
157→            } else {
158→                Intent(this, Reviewer::class.java).apply {
159→                    addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
160→                }
161→            }
162→        CollectionManager.getColUnsafe().decks.select(deckId)
163→        // Clean the stack out under the reviewer to avoid any incorrect activities / dialogs /
164→        // data state from prior app usage showing after reviewer exits if going to reviewer directly
165→        TaskStackBuilder
166→            .create(applicationContext)
167→            .addNextIntent(reloadIntent)
168→            .addNextIntent(reviewIntent)
169→            .startActivities()
170→        finish()
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun handleSyncIntent(
174→        reloadIntent: Intent,
175→        action: String?,
176→    ) {
177→        Timber.i("Handling Sync Intent")
178→        sendDoSyncMsg()
179→        reloadIntent.action = action
180→        reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
181→        startActivity(reloadIntent)
182→        finish()
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun handleFileImport(
186→        intent: Intent,
187→        reloadIntent: Intent,
188→        action: String?,
189→    ) {
190→        Timber.i("Handling file import")
191→        if (!hasShownAppIntro()) {
192→            Timber.i("Trying to import a file when the app was not started at all")
193→            showThemedToast(this, R.string.app_not_initialized_new, false)
194→            return
195→        }
196→        val importResult = handleFileImport(this, intent)
197→        // attempt to delete the downloaded deck if it is a shared deck download import
198→        if (intent.hasExtra(SharedDecksDownloadFragment.EXTRA_IS_SHARED_DOWNLOAD)) {
199→            deleteDownloadedDeck(intent.data)
200→        }
201→
202→        // Start DeckPicker if we correctly processed ACTION_VIEW
203→        if (importResult.isSuccess) {
204→            deleteImportedDeck(intent.data?.path)
205→            reloadIntent.action = action
206→            reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
207→            startActivity(reloadIntent)
208→            finish()
209→        } else {
210→            Timber.i("File import failed")
211→            // Don't import the file if it didn't load properly or doesn't have apkg extension
212→            showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, true)
213→        }
214→    }
215→
216→    private fun deleteImportedDeck(path: String?) {
217→        try {
218→            val file = File(path!!)
219→            val fileUri =
220→                applicationContext?.let {
221→                    FileProvider.getUriForFile(
222→                        it,
223→                        it.applicationContext?.packageName + ".apkgfileprovider",
224→                        File(it.getExternalFilesDir(FileUtil.getDownloadDirectory()), file.name),
225→                    )
226→                }
227→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
228→            contentResolver.delete(fileUri!!, null, null)
229→            Timber.i("onCreate() import successful and downloaded file deleted")
230→        } catch (e: Exception) {
231→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate() import successful and cannot delete file")
232→        }
233→    }
234→
235→    private fun handleImageImport(data: Intent) {
236→        val imageUri =
237→            if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
238→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri::class.java)
239→            } else {
240→                data.data
241→            }
242→
243→        val imageOcclusionIntentBuilder = ImageOcclusionIntentBuilder(this)
244→        val intentImageOcclusion = imageOcclusionIntentBuilder.buildIntent(imageUri)
245→
246→        TaskStackBuilder
247→            .create(this)
248→            .addNextIntentWithParentStack(Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java))
249→            .addNextIntent(intentImageOcclusion)
250→            .startActivities()
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun deleteDownloadedDeck(sharedDeckUri: Uri?) {
254→        if (sharedDeckUri == null) {
255→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded a shared deck but uri was null when trying to delete its file")
256→            return
257→        }
258→
259→        try {
260→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
261→            contentResolver.delete(sharedDeckUri, null, null)
262→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded shared deck deleted")
263→        } catch (e: Exception) {
264→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate: failed to delete downloaded shared deck")
265→        }
266→    }
267→
268→    private fun launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent: Intent) {
269→        // Launcher intents should start DeckPicker if no other task exists,
270→        // otherwise go to previous task
271→        Timber.i("Launching DeckPicker")
272→        reloadIntent.action = Intent.ACTION_MAIN
273→        reloadIntent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_LAUNCHER)
274→        reloadIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
275→        startActivityIfNeeded(reloadIntent, 0)
276→        finish()
277→    }
278→
279→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: Also extract the parameters into here to reduce coupling
280→    @VisibleForTesting
281→    enum class LaunchType {
282→        DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
283→
284→        /** colpkg/apkg/unknown */
285→        FILE_IMPORT,
286→
287→        /** csv/tsv */
288→        TEXT_IMPORT,
289→
290→        /** image */
291→        IMAGE_IMPORT,
292→
293→        SYNC,
294→        REVIEW,
295→        COPY_DEBUG_INFO,
296→    }
297→
298→    companion object {
299→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT = "com.ichi2.anki.COPY_DEBUG_INFO"
300→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA = "clip_data"
301→
302→        private fun isValidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean {
303→            // Negating a negative because we want to call specific attention to the fact that it's invalid
304→            // #6312 - Smart Launcher provided an empty ACTION_VIEW, no point in importing here.
305→            return !isInvalidViewIntent(intent)
306→        }
307→
308→        /** Checks whether storage permissions are granted on the device. If the device is not using legacy storage,
309→         *  it verifies if the app has been granted the necessary storage access permission.
310→         *  @return `true`: if granted, otherwise `false` and shows a missing permission toast
311→         */
312→        fun grantedStoragePermissions(
313→            context: Context,
314→            showToast: Boolean,
315→        ): Boolean {
316→            val granted =
317→                !ScopedStorageService.isLegacyStorage(context) ||
318→                    hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission(context) ||
319→                    Permissions.isExternalStorageManagerCompat()
320→
321→            if (!granted && showToast) {
322→                showThemedToast(context, context.getString(R.string.intent_handler_failed_no_storage_permission), false)
323→            }
324→
325→            return granted
326→        }
327→
328→        @VisibleForTesting
329→        @CheckResult
330→        fun getLaunchType(intent: Intent): LaunchType {
331→            val action = intent.action
332→            return if (action == Intent.ACTION_SEND || Intent.ACTION_VIEW == action && isValidViewIntent(intent)) {
333→                val mimeType = intent.resolveMimeType()
334→                when {
335→                    mimeType?.startsWith("image/") == true -> LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT
336→                    mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
337→                        mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
338→                    else -> LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT
339→                }
340→            } else if ("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" == action) {
341→                LaunchType.SYNC
342→            } else if (intent.hasExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID)) {
343→                LaunchType.REVIEW
344→            } else if (action == CLIPBOARD_INTENT) {
345→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO
346→            } else {
347→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW
348→            }
349→        }
350→
351→        /**
352→         * Send a Message to AnkiDroidApp so that the DialogMessageHandler forces a sync
353→         */
354→        fun sendDoSyncMsg() {
355→            // Store the message in AnkiDroidApp message holder, which is loaded later in AnkiActivity.onResume
356→            storeMessage(DoSync().toMessage())
357→        }
358→
359→        fun copyStringToClipboardIntent(
360→            context: Context,
361→            textToCopy: String,
362→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).also {
363→            it.action = CLIPBOARD_INTENT
364→            // max length for an intent is 500KB.
365→            // 25000 * 2 (bytes per char) = 50,000 bytes <<< 500KB
366→            it.putExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA, textToCopy.trimToLength(25000))
367→        }
368→
369→        fun requiresCollectionAccess(launchType: LaunchType): Boolean =
370→            when (launchType) {
371→                LaunchType.SYNC,
372→                LaunchType.REVIEW,
373→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
374→                LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT,
375→                LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT,
376→                LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT,
377→                -> true
378→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> false
379→            }
380→
381→        class DoSync :
382→            DialogHandlerMessage(
383→                which = WhichDialogHandler.MSG_DO_SYNC,
384→                analyticName = "DoSyncDialog",
385→            ) {
386→            override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
387→                // we may be called via any AnkiActivity but sync is a DeckPicker thing
388→                if (activity !is DeckPicker) {
389→                    showError(
390→                        activity,
391→                        activity.getString(R.string.something_wrong),
392→                        ClassCastException(activity.javaClass.simpleName + " is not " + DeckPicker.javaClass.simpleName),
393→                        true,
394→                    )
395→                    return
396→                }
397→                // let's be clear about the type now that we've checked
398→                val deckPicker = activity
399→
400→                val preferences = deckPicker.sharedPrefs()
401→                val res = deckPicker.resources
402→                val hkey = preferences.getString("hkey", "")
403→                val millisecondsSinceLastSync = millisecondsSinceLastSync(preferences)
404→                val limited = millisecondsSinceLastSync < INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL
405→                if (!limited && hkey!!.isNotEmpty() && NetworkUtils.isOnline) {
406→                    deckPicker.sync()
407→                } else {
408→                    val err = res.getString(R.string.sync_error)
409→                    if (limited) {
410→                        val remainingTimeInSeconds =
411→                            max((INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL - millisecondsSinceLastSync) / 1000, 1)
412→                        // getQuantityString needs an int
413→                        val remaining = min(Int.MAX_VALUE.toLong(), remainingTimeInSeconds).toInt()
414→                        val message =
415→                            res.getQuantityString(
416→                                R.plurals.sync_automatic_sync_needs_more_time,
417→                                remaining,
418→                                remaining,
419→                            )
420→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(err, message, Channel.SYNC)
421→                    } else {
422→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(
423→                            err,
424→                            res.getString(R.string.youre_offline),
425→                            Channel.SYNC,
426→                        )
427→                    }
428→                }
429→                deckPicker.finish()
430→            }
431→
432→            override fun toMessage(): Message = emptyMessage(this.what)
433→
434→            companion object {
435→                const val INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL =
436→                    (
437→                        2 * 60000 // 2min minimum sync interval
438→                    ).toLong()
439→            }
440→        }
441→
442→        /**
443→         * Returns an intent to review a specific deck.
444→         * This does not states which reviewer to use, instead IntentHandler will choose whether to use the
445→         * legacy or the new reviewer based on the "newReviewer" preference.
446→         * It is expected to be used from widget, shortcut, reminders but not from ankidroid directly because of the CLEAR_TOP flag.
447→         */
448→        fun intentToReviewDeckFromShorcuts(
449→            context: Context,
450→            deckId: DeckId,
451→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).apply {
452→            setAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
453→            putExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, deckId)
454→        }
455→    }
456→}

 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.utils
 18→
 19→import android.app.Activity
 20→import android.content.ContentResolver
 21→import android.content.Context
 22→import android.content.Intent
 23→import android.net.Uri
 24→import android.os.Message
 25→import android.provider.OpenableColumns
 26→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 27→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 28→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.CrashReportService
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandler
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandlerMessage
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.ImportDialog
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.showImportDialog
 37→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 38→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 39→import org.jetbrains.annotations.Contract
 40→import timber.log.Timber
 41→import java.io.File
 42→import java.io.FileNotFoundException
 43→import java.io.IOException
 44→import java.io.InputStream
 45→import java.net.URLDecoder
 46→import java.net.URLEncoder
 47→import java.util.Locale
 48→
 49→object ImportUtils {
 50→    // A filename should be shortened if over this threshold
 51→    private const val FILE_NAME_SHORTENING_THRESHOLD = 100
 52→
 53→    /**
 54→     * This code is used in multiple places to handle package imports
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context for use in resource resolution and path finding
 57→     * @param intent contains the file to import
 58→     * @return null if successful, otherwise error message
 59→     */
 60→    fun handleFileImport(
 61→        context: Context,
 62→        intent: Intent,
 63→    ): ImportResult = FileImporter().handleFileImport(context, intent)
 64→
 65→    /**
 66→     * Makes a cached copy of the file selected on [intent] and returns its path
 67→     */
 68→    fun getFileCachedCopy(
 69→        context: Context,
 70→        intent: Intent,
 71→    ): String? = FileImporter().getFileCachedCopy(context, intent)
 72→
 73→    fun getFileCachedCopy(
 74→        context: Context,
 75→        uri: Uri,
 76→    ): String? = FileImporter().getFileCachedCopy(context, uri)
 77→
 78→    fun showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(
 79→        activity: Activity,
 80→        errorMessage: String?,
 81→        exitActivity: Boolean,
 82→    ) {
 83→        FileImporter().showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(activity, errorMessage, exitActivity)
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    fun isCollectionPackage(filename: String?): Boolean =
 87→        filename != null && (filename.lowercase(Locale.ROOT).endsWith(".colpkg") || "collection.apkg" == filename)
 88→
 89→    /** @return Whether the file is either a deck, or a collection package */
 90→    @Contract("null -> false")
 91→    fun isValidPackageName(filename: String?): Boolean = FileImporter.isDeckPackage(filename) || isCollectionPackage(filename)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Whether importUtils can handle the given intent
 95→     * Caused by #6312 - A launcher was sending ACTION_VIEW instead of ACTION_MAIN
 96→     */
 97→    fun isInvalidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean = intent.data == null && intent.clipData == null
 98→
 99→    fun isFileAValidDeck(fileName: String): Boolean =
100→        FileImporter.hasExtension(fileName, "apkg") || FileImporter.hasExtension(fileName, "colpkg")
101→
102→    @SuppressWarnings("WeakerAccess")
103→    open class FileImporter {
104→        /**
105→         * This code is used in multiple places to handle package imports
106→         *
107→         * @param context for use in resource resolution and path finding
108→         * @param intent contains the file to import
109→         * @return null if successful, otherwise error message
110→         */
111→        fun handleFileImport(
112→            context: Context,
113→            intent: Intent,
114→        ): ImportResult {
115→            // This intent is used for opening apkg package files
116→            // We want to go immediately to DeckPicker, clearing any history in the process
117→            Timber.i("IntentHandler/ User requested to view a file")
118→            val extras = if (intent.extras == null) "none" else intent.extras!!.keySet().joinToString(", ")
119→            Timber.i("Intent: %s. Data: %s", intent, extras)
120→            return try {
121→                handleFileImportInternal(context, intent)
122→            } catch (e: Exception) {
123→                CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(e, "handleFileImport")
124→                Timber.e(e, "failed to handle import intent")
125→                ImportResult.fromErrorString(context.getString(R.string.import_error_handle_exception, e.localizedMessage))
126→            }
127→        }
128→
129→        private fun handleFileImportInternal(
130→            context: Context,
131→            intent: Intent,
132→        ): ImportResult {
133→            val importPathUri = getDataUri(intent)
134→            return if (importPathUri != null) {
135→                handleContentProviderFile(context, importPathUri, intent)
136→            } else {
137→                ImportResult.fromErrorString(context.getString(R.string.import_error_handle_exception))
138→            }
139→        }
140→
141→        @NeedsTest("Check file name is absolute")
142→        fun getFileCachedCopy(
143→            context: Context,
144→            uri: Uri,
145→        ): String? {
146→            val filename = ensureValidLength(getFileNameFromContentProvider(context, uri) ?: return null)
147→            val tempFile = File(context.cacheDir, filename)
148→            return if (copyFileToCache(context, uri, tempFile.absolutePath).first) {
149→                tempFile.absolutePath
150→            } else {
151→                null
152→            }
153→        }
154→
155→        /**
156→         * Makes a cached copy of the file selected on [intent] and returns its path
157→         */
158→        fun getFileCachedCopy(
159→            context: Context,
160→            intent: Intent,
161→        ): String? {
162→            val uri = getDataUri(intent) ?: return null
163→            return getFileCachedCopy(context, uri)
164→        }
165→
166→        fun handleContentProviderFile(
167→            context: Context,
168→            importPathUri: Uri,
169→            intent: Intent? = null,
170→        ): ImportResult {
171→            // Note: intent.getData() can be null. Use data instead.
172→            if (!isValidImportType(context, importPathUri)) {
173→                return ImportResult.fromErrorString(context.getString(R.string.import_log_no_apkg))
174→            }
175→            // Get the original filename from the content provider URI
176→            var filename = getFileNameFromContentProvider(context, importPathUri)
177→            // Hack to fix bug where ContentResolver not returning filename correctly
178→            if (filename == null) {
179→                when (intent?.type) {
180→                    "application/apkg", "application/zip" -> {
181→                        // Set a dummy filename if MIME type provided or is a valid zip file
182→                        filename = "unknown_filename.apkg"
183→                        Timber.w("Could not retrieve filename from ContentProvider, but was valid zip file so we try to continue")
184→                    }
185→
186→                    else -> {
187→                        Timber.e("Could not retrieve filename from ContentProvider")
188→                        CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(
189→                            RuntimeException("Could not import apkg from ContentProvider"),
190→                            "IntentHandler.java",
191→                            "apkg import failed; mime type ${intent?.type}",
192→                        )
193→                        return ImportResult.fromErrorString(
194→                            AnkiDroidApp.appResources.getString(
195→                                R.string.import_error_content_provider,
196→                                AnkiDroidApp.manualUrl + "#importing",
197→                            ),
198→                        )
199→                    }
200→                }
201→            }
202→            val tempOutDir: String
203→            if (!isValidPackageName(filename)) {
204→                return if (isAnkiDatabase(filename)) {
205→                    // .anki2 files aren't supported by Anki Desktop, we should eventually support them, because we can
206→                    // but for now, show a "nice" error.
207→                    ImportResult.fromErrorString(context.resources.getString(R.string.import_error_load_imported_database))
208→                } else {
209→                    // Don't import if file doesn't have an Anki package extension
210→                    ImportResult.fromErrorString(context.resources.getString(R.string.import_error_not_apkg_extension, filename))
211→                }
212→            } else {
213→                // Copy to temporary file
214→                filename = ensureValidLength(filename)
215→                tempOutDir = Uri.fromFile(File(context.cacheDir, filename)).encodedPath!!
216→                val cachingResult = copyFileToCache(context, importPathUri, tempOutDir)
217→                val errorMessage =
218→                    if (cachingResult.first) {
219→                        null
220→                    } else {
221→                        context.getString(R.string.import_error_copy_to_cache)
222→                    }
223→                // Show import dialog
224→                if (errorMessage != null) {
225→                    CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(
226→                        RuntimeException("Error importing apkg file"),
227→                        "IntentHandler.java",
228→                        cachingResult.second,
229→                    )
230→                    return ImportResult.fromErrorString(errorMessage)
231→                }
232→            }
233→            sendShowImportFileDialogMsg(tempOutDir)
234→            return ImportResult.fromSuccess()
235→        }
236→
237→        fun isValidImportType(
238→            context: Context,
239→            importPathUri: Uri,
240→        ): Boolean {
241→            val fileName = getFileNameFromContentProvider(context, importPathUri)
242→            return when {
243→                isDeckPackage(fileName) -> true
244→                isCollectionPackage(fileName) -> true
245→                else -> false
246→            }
247→        }
248→
249→        private fun isAnkiDatabase(filename: String?): Boolean = filename != null && hasExtension(filename, "anki2")
250→
251→        private fun ensureValidLength(fileName: String): String {
252→            // #6137 - filenames can be too long when URLEncoded
253→            return try {
254→                val encoded = URLEncoder.encode(fileName, "UTF-8")
255→                if (encoded.length <= FILE_NAME_SHORTENING_THRESHOLD) {
256→                    Timber.d("No filename truncation necessary")
257→                    fileName
258→                } else {
259→                    Timber.d("Filename was longer than %d, shortening", FILE_NAME_SHORTENING_THRESHOLD)
260→                    // take 90 instead of 100 so we don't get the extension
261→                    val substringLength = FILE_NAME_SHORTENING_THRESHOLD - 10
262→                    val shortenedFileName = encoded.substring(0, substringLength) + "..." + getExtension(fileName)
263→                    Timber.d("Shortened filename '%s' to '%s'", fileName, shortenedFileName)
264→                    // if we don't decode, % is double-encoded
265→                    URLDecoder.decode(shortenedFileName, "UTF-8")
266→                }
267→            } catch (e: Exception) {
268→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to shorten file: %s", fileName)
269→                fileName
270→            }
271→        }
272→
273→        @CheckResult
274→        private fun getExtension(fileName: String): String {
275→            val file = Uri.fromFile(File(fileName))
276→            return AssetHelper.getFileExtensionFromFilePath(file.toString())
277→        }
278→
279→        protected open fun getFileNameFromContentProvider(
280→            context: Context,
281→            data: Uri,
282→        ): String? {
283→            var filename: String? = null
284→            try {
285→                context.contentResolver
286→                    .query(
287→                        data,
288→                        arrayOf(OpenableColumns.DISPLAY_NAME),
289→                        null,
290→                        null,
291→                        null,
292→                    ).use { cursor ->
293→                        if (cursor != null && cursor.moveToFirst()) {
294→                            filename = cursor.getString(0)
295→                            Timber.d("handleFileImport() Importing from content provider: %s", filename)
296→                        }
297→                    }
298→            } catch (e: Exception) {
299→                Timber.w(e, "Error querying content provider")
300→                filename = null // Set filename to null in case of an exception
301→            }
302→            return filename
303→        }
304→
305→        fun showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(
306→            activity: Activity,
307→            errorMessage: String?,
308→            exitActivity: Boolean,
309→        ) {
310→            Timber.e("showImportUnsuccessfulDialog() message %s", errorMessage)
311→            val title = activity.resources.getString(R.string.import_title_error)
312→            AlertDialog.Builder(activity).show {
313→                title(text = title)
314→                message(text = errorMessage!!)
315→                setCancelable(false)
316→                positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
317→                    if (exitActivity) {
318→                        activity.finish()
319→                    }
320→                }
321→            }
322→        }
323→
324→        /**
325→         * Copy the data from the intent to a temporary file
326→         * @param data intent from which to get input stream
327→         * @param tempPath temporary path to store the cached file
328→         * @return a [Pair] with a boolean indicating if the copy to cache action was successful
329→         * and an optional message if anything went wrong
330→         */
331→        protected open fun copyFileToCache(
332→            context: Context,
333→            data: Uri?,
334→            tempPath: String,
335→        ): Pair<Boolean, String?> {
336→            // Get an input stream to the data in ContentProvider
337→            val inputStream: InputStream =
338→                try {
339→                    context.contentResolver.openInputStream(data!!)
340→                } catch (e: FileNotFoundException) {
341→                    Timber.e(e, "Could not open input stream to intent data")
342→                    return Pair(false, "copyFileToCache: FileNotFoundException when accessing ContentProvider")
343→                } ?: return Pair(false, "copyFileToCache: provider recently crashed")
344→            // Check non-null
345→            try {
346→                CompatHelper.compat.copyFile(inputStream, tempPath)
347→            } catch (e: IOException) {
348→                Timber.e(e, "Could not copy file to %s", tempPath)
349→                return Pair(false, "copyFileToCache: ${e.javaClass} when copying the imported file")
350→            } finally {
351→                try {
352→                    inputStream.close()
353→                } catch (e: IOException) {
354→                    Timber.e(e, "Error closing input stream")
355→                }
356→            }
357→            return Pair(true, null)
358→        }
359→
360→        companion object {
361→            fun getDataUri(intent: Intent): Uri? {
362→                if (intent.data == null) {
363→                    Timber.i("No intent data. Attempting to read clip data.")
364→                    if (intent.clipData == null || intent.clipData!!.itemCount == 0) {
365→                        return null
366→                    }
367→                    return intent.clipData?.getItemAt(0)?.uri
368→                }
369→                // If Uri is of scheme which is supported by ContentResolver, read the contents
370→                val intentUriScheme = intent.data!!.scheme
371→                return when (intentUriScheme) {
372→                    ContentResolver.SCHEME_CONTENT,
373→                    ContentResolver.SCHEME_FILE,
374→                    ContentResolver.SCHEME_ANDROID_RESOURCE,
375→                    -> {
376→                        Timber.i("Attempting to read data from intent.")
377→                        intent.data
378→                    }
379→                    else -> null
380→                }
381→            }
382→
383→            /**
384→             * Send a Message to AnkiDroidApp so that the DialogMessageHandler shows the Import apkg dialog.
385→             * @param importPath path of to apkg file which will be imported
386→             */
387→            private fun sendShowImportFileDialogMsg(importPath: String) {
388→                // Get the filename from the path
389→                val filename = File(importPath).name
390→
391→                val dialogMessage =
392→                    if (isCollectionPackage(filename)) {
393→                        CollectionImportReplace(importPath)
394→                    } else {
395→                        CollectionImportAdd(importPath)
396→                    }
397→                // Store the message in AnkiDroidApp message holder, which is loaded later in AnkiActivity.onResume
398→                DialogHandler.storeMessage(dialogMessage.toMessage())
399→            }
400→
401→            internal fun isDeckPackage(filename: String?): Boolean =
402→                filename != null && filename.lowercase(Locale.ROOT).endsWith(".apkg") && "collection.apkg" != filename
403→
404→            fun hasExtension(
405→                filename: String,
406→                extension: String?,
407→            ): Boolean {
408→                val fileParts = filename.split("\\.".toRegex()).toTypedArray()
409→                if (fileParts.size < 2) {
410→                    return false
411→                }
412→                val extensionSegment = fileParts[fileParts.size - 1]
413→                // either "apkg", or "apkg (1)".
414→                // COULD_BE_BETTE: accepts .apkgaa"
415→                return extensionSegment.lowercase(Locale.ROOT).startsWith(extension!!)
416→            }
417→        }
418→    }
419→
420→    class ImportResult(
421→        val humanReadableMessage: String?,
422→    ) {
423→        val isSuccess: Boolean
424→            get() = humanReadableMessage == null
425→
426→        companion object {
427→            fun fromErrorString(message: String?): ImportResult = ImportResult(message)
428→
429→            fun fromSuccess(): ImportResult = ImportResult(null)
430→        }
431→    }
432→
433→    /** Show confirmation dialog asking to confirm import with replace when file called "collection.apkg" */
434→    class CollectionImportReplace(
435→        private val importPath: String,
436→    ) : DialogHandlerMessage(
437→            which = WhichDialogHandler.MSG_SHOW_COLLECTION_IMPORT_REPLACE_DIALOG,
438→            analyticName = "ImportReplaceDialog",
439→        ) {
440→        override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
441→            // Handle import of collection package APKG
442→            activity.showImportDialog(ImportDialog.Type.DIALOG_IMPORT_REPLACE_CONFIRM, importPath)
443→        }
444→
445→        override fun toMessage(): Message =
446→            Message.obtain().apply {
447→                data = bundleOf("importPath" to importPath)
448→                what = this@CollectionImportReplace.what
449→            }
450→
451→        companion object {
452→            fun fromMessage(message: Message): CollectionImportReplace = CollectionImportReplace(message.data.getString("importPath")!!)
453→        }
454→    }
455→
456→    /** Show confirmation dialog asking to confirm import with add */
457→    class CollectionImportAdd(
458→        private val importPath: String,
459→    ) : DialogHandlerMessage(
460→            WhichDialogHandler.MSG_SHOW_COLLECTION_IMPORT_ADD_DIALOG,
461→            "ImportAddDialog",
462→        ) {
463→        override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
464→            // Handle import of deck package APKG
465→            activity.showImportDialog(ImportDialog.Type.DIALOG_IMPORT_ADD_CONFIRM, importPath)
466→        }
467→
468→        override fun toMessage(): Message =
469→            Message.obtain().apply {
470→                data = bundleOf("importPath" to importPath)
471→                what = this@CollectionImportAdd.what
472→            }
473→
474→        companion object {
475→            fun fromMessage(message: Message): CollectionImportAdd = CollectionImportAdd(message.data.getString("importPath")!!)
476→        }
477→    }
478→}

 35→
 36→// see also:
 37→// ImportFileSelectionFragment - selects 'APKG/COLPKG/CSV' and opens a file picker
 38→// onSelectedPackageToImport/onSelectedCsvForImport
 39→// importUtils - copying selected file into local cache
 40→// ImportDialog - confirmation screen after file copied to cache
 41→//    * ImportDialogListener - AnkiActivity implementation of handler for the confirmation screen
 42→//    * AnkiActivity.importAdd/importReplace - called from confirmation screen
 43→// BackendBackups/BackendImporting - new backend for importing
 44→// importReplaceListener - old backend listener for importing
 45→
 46→fun interface ImportColpkgListener {
 47→    fun onImportColpkg(colpkgPath: String?)
 48→}
 49→
 50→@NeedsTest("successful import from the app menu")
 51→fun AnkiActivity.onSelectedPackageToImport(data: Intent) {
 52→    val importResult = ImportUtils.handleFileImport(this, data)
 53→    if (!importResult.isSuccess) {
 54→        runOnUiThread {
 55→            ImportUtils.showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, false)
 56→        }
 57→    } else {
 58→        // a Message was posted, don't wait for onResume to process it
 59→        if (this.lifecycle.currentState.isAtLeast(Lifecycle.State.RESUMED)) {
 60→            dialogHandler.popMessage()?.let { dialogHandler.sendStoredMessage(it) }
 61→        }
 62→    }
 63→}
 64→
 65→fun Activity.onSelectedCsvForImport(data: Intent) {
 66→    val path = ImportUtils.getFileCachedCopy(this, data) ?: return
 67→    val csvImporterIntent = CsvImporter.getIntent(this, path)
 68→
 69→    val stackBuilder = TaskStackBuilder.create(this)
 70→    stackBuilder.addNextIntentWithParentStack(Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java))
 71→    stackBuilder.addNextIntent(csvImporterIntent)
 72→
 73→    stackBuilder.startActivities()
 74→}
 75→
 76→fun AnkiActivity.showImportDialog(
 77→    id: ImportDialog.Type,
 78→    importPath: String,
 79→) {
 80→    Timber.d("showImportDialog() delegating to ImportDialog")
 81→    val newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment = ImportDialog.newInstance(id, importPath)
 82→    showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment)
 83→}
 84→
 85→fun AnkiActivity.showImportDialog() {
 86→    showImportDialog(
 87→        ImportOptions(
 88→            importApkg = true,
 89→            importColpkg = true,
 90→            importTextFile = true,
 91→        ),
 92→    )
 93→}
 94→
 95→fun AnkiActivity.showImportDialog(options: ImportOptions) {
 96→    showDialogFragment(ImportFileSelectionFragment.newInstance(options))
 97→}
 98→
 99→class DatabaseRestorationListener(
100→    val activity: AnkiActivity,
101→    val newAnkiDroidDirectory: String,
102→) : ImportColpkgListener {
103→    override fun onImportColpkg(colpkgPath: String?) {
104→        Timber.i("Database restoration correct")
105→        activity.sharedPrefs().edit {
106→            putString("deckPath", newAnkiDroidDirectory)
107→        }
108→        activity.dismissAllDialogFragments()
109→        activity.importColpkgListener = null
110→        CollectionHelper.ankiDroidDirectoryOverride = null
111→    }
112→}

1→/*
  2→ Copyright (c) 2020 David Allison <davidallisongithub@gmail.com>
  3→
  4→ This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under
  5→ the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software
  6→ Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later
  7→ version.
  8→
  9→ This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY
 10→ WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
 11→ PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
 12→
 13→ You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14→ this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.net.Uri
 21→import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler.Companion.getLaunchType
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.IntentHandler.LaunchType
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService.Companion.getReviewDeckIntent
 25→import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
 26→import org.hamcrest.Matchers.equalTo
 27→import org.junit.Test
 28→import org.junit.runner.RunWith
 29→import org.mockito.Mockito
 30→
 31→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
 32→class IntentHandlerTest {
 33→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: We're testing class internals here, would like to see these tests be replaced with
 34→    // higher-level tests at a later date when we better extract dependencies
 35→    @Test
 36→    fun viewIntentReturnsView() {
 37→        var intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, Uri.parse("content://invalid"))
 38→        var expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 39→
 40→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT))
 41→
 42→        intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_SEND, Uri.parse("content://invalid"))
 43→        expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 44→
 45→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT))
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    @Test
 49→    fun syncIntentReturnsSync() {
 50→        val intent = Intent("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC")
 51→
 52→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 53→
 54→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.SYNC))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    @Test
 58→    fun reviewIntentReturnsReview() {
 59→        val intent = getReviewDeckIntent(Mockito.mock(Context::class.java), 1)
 60→
 61→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 62→
 63→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.REVIEW))
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    @Test
 67→    fun mainIntentStartsApp() {
 68→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_MAIN)
 69→
 70→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 71→
 72→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW))
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    @Test
 76→    fun imageOcclusionIntent() {
 77→        val mimeTypes = listOf("image/jpeg", "image/png")
 78→
 79→        for (mimeType in mimeTypes) {
 80→            var intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 81→            intent.setDataAndType(Uri.parse("content://valid"), mimeType)
 82→            var expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 83→
 84→            assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT))
 85→
 86→            intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_SEND)
 87→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri.parse("content://valid"))
 88→            intent.type = mimeType
 89→            expected = getLaunchType(intent)
 90→
 91→            assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT))
 92→        }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    @Test
 96→    fun textImportIntentReturnsTextImport() {
 97→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/tab-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT)
 98→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/comma-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT)
 99→
100→        // Test for ACTION_SEND
101→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/tab-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT, Intent.ACTION_SEND)
102→        testIntentType("content://valid", "text/comma-separated-values", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT, Intent.ACTION_SEND)
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun testIntentType(
106→        data: String,
107→        type: String,
108→        expected: LaunchType,
109→        action: String = Intent.ACTION_VIEW,
110→    ) {
111→        val intent = Intent(action)
112→        intent.setDataAndType(Uri.parse(data), type)
113→        val actual = getLaunchType(intent)
114→        assertThat(actual, equalTo(expected))
115→    }
116→
117→    @Test
118→    fun viewWithNoDataPerformsDefaultAction() {
119→        // #6312 - Smart Launcher double-tap launches us with this. No data at all in the intent
120→        // so we can only perform the default action
121→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
122→
123→        val expected = getLaunchType(intent)
124→
125→        assertThat(expected, equalTo(LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW))
126→    }
127→}
128→

1→/*

 11→ *  PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.
 12→ *
 13→ *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.dialogs
 18→
 19→import android.app.Dialog
 20→import android.content.ActivityNotFoundException
 21→import android.content.Intent
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Parcelable
 24→import androidx.activity.result.ActivityResultLauncher
 25→import androidx.annotation.StringRes
 26→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 27→import androidx.core.os.BundleCompat
 28→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 29→import androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 34→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.TEXT_PLAIN
 36→import com.ichi2.utils.title
 37→import kotlinx.parcelize.Parcelize
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@NeedsTest("Selecting APKG does not allow multiple files")
 41→@NeedsTest("Selecting COLPKG does not allow multiple files")
 42→@NeedsTest("Restore backup dialog does not allow multiple files")
 43→class ImportFileSelectionFragment : DialogFragment() {
 44→    override fun onCreateDialog(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): Dialog {
 45→        val entries = buildImportEntries()
 46→        return AlertDialog
 47→            .Builder(requireActivity())
 48→            .title(R.string.menu_import)
 49→            .setItems(
 50→                entries.map { requireActivity().getString(it.titleRes) }.toTypedArray(),
 51→            ) { _, position ->
 52→                val entry = entries[position]
 53→                UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 54→                    UsageAnalytics.Category.LINK_CLICKED,
 55→                    entry.analyticsId,
 56→                )
 57→                openImportFilePicker(
 58→                    activity = requireActivity() as AnkiActivity,
 59→                    fileType = entry.type,
 60→                    multiple = entry.multiple,
 61→                    mimeType = entry.mimeType,
 62→                    extraMimes = entry.extraMimes,
 63→                )
 64→            }.create()
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    private fun buildImportEntries(): List<ImportEntry> {
 68→        return arguments?.let { args ->
 69→            args.classLoader = this@ImportFileSelectionFragment::class.java.classLoader
 70→            val options =
 71→                BundleCompat.getParcelable(args, ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS, ImportOptions::class.java)
 72→                    ?: return emptyList()
 73→            mutableListOf<ImportEntry>().apply {
 74→                if (options.importApkg) {
 75→                    add(
 76→                        ImportEntry(
 77→                            R.string.import_deck_package,
 78→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_APKG_FILE,
 79→                            ImportFileType.APKG,
 80→                        ),
 81→                    )
 82→                }
 83→                if (options.importColpkg) {
 84→                    add(
 85→                        ImportEntry(
 86→                            R.string.import_collection_package,
 87→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_COLPKG_FILE,
 88→                            ImportFileType.COLPKG,
 89→                        ),
 90→                    )
 91→                }
 92→                if (options.importTextFile) {
 93→                    add(
 94→                        ImportEntry(
 95→                            R.string.import_csv,
 96→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_CSV_FILE,
 97→                            ImportFileType.CSV,
 98→                            multiple = false,
 99→                            mimeType = "*/*",
100→                            extraMimes =
101→                                arrayOf(
102→                                    TEXT_PLAIN,
103→                                    "text/comma-separated-values",
104→                                    "text/csv",
105→                                    "text/tab-separated-values",
106→                                ),
107→                        ),
108→                    )
109→                }
110→            }
111→        } ?: emptyList()
112→    }
113→
114→    // safe as this data class is used as a container and it's not involved in any comparing
115→    @Suppress("ArrayInDataClass")
116→    private data class ImportEntry(
117→        @StringRes val titleRes: Int,
118→        val analyticsId: String,
119→        val type: ImportFileType,
120→        var multiple: Boolean = false,
121→        val mimeType: String = "*/*",
122→        val extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
123→    )
124→
125→    @Parcelize
126→    data class ImportOptions(
127→        val importColpkg: Boolean,
128→        val importApkg: Boolean,
129→        val importTextFile: Boolean,
130→    ) : Parcelable
131→
132→    enum class ImportFileType {
133→        APKG,
134→        COLPKG,
135→        CSV,
136→    }
137→
138→    interface ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider {
139→        fun getApkgFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
140→    }
141→
142→    interface CsvImportResultLauncherProvider {
143→        fun getCsvFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
144→    }
145→
146→    companion object {
147→        private const val ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS = "arg_import_options"
148→
149→        fun newInstance(options: ImportOptions) =
150→            ImportFileSelectionFragment().apply {
151→                arguments = bundleOf(ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS to options)
152→            }
153→
154→        /**
155→         * Calls through the system with an [Intent] to pick a file to be imported.
156→         */
157→        fun openImportFilePicker(
158→            activity: AnkiActivity,
159→            fileType: ImportFileType,
160→            multiple: Boolean = false,
161→            mimeType: String = "*/*",
162→            extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
163→        ) {
164→            Timber.d("openImportFilePicker() delegating to file picker intent")
165→            val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_OPEN_DOCUMENT)
166→            intent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_OPENABLE)
167→            intent.type = mimeType
168→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_ADVANCED", true)
169→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.FANCY", true)
170→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_FILESIZE", true)
171→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_ALLOW_MULTIPLE, multiple)
172→            extraMimes?.let { intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_MIME_TYPES, it) }
173→
174→            try {
175→                if (
176→                    (fileType == ImportFileType.APKG || fileType == ImportFileType.COLPKG) &&
177→                    activity is ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider
178→                ) {
179→                    activity.getApkgFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
180→                } else if (fileType == ImportFileType.CSV && activity is CsvImportResultLauncherProvider) {
181→                    activity.getCsvFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
182→                } else {
183→                    Timber.w("Activity($activity) can't handle requested import: $fileType")
184→                }
185→            } catch (ex: ActivityNotFoundException) {
186→                Timber.w("No activity to handle openImportFilePicker request")
187→                activity.showSnackbar(R.string.activity_start_failed)
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→}
192→
